libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No environment or system-property setup is indicated. Generated tests should target Spring Boot Actuator 3.0 APIs rather than reusing removed or changed 2.x APIs, for example use org.springframework.boot.actuate.web.exchanges.* instead of the removed org.springframework.boot.actuate.trace.http.* types, and use the 3.0 constructors for HealthEndpoint, EnvironmentEndpoint, Sanitizer, and BeansEndpoint.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8867 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-actuator:3.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: spring-boot-actuator has many optional integration classes for web, security, reactive, data stores, messaging, Jackson, and Micrometer; tests should avoid optional integration surfaces unless they intentionally add matching dependencies.

The recommended application-level usage is spring-boot-starter-actuator, but this issue targets the core org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-actuator artifact, whose direct endpoint APIs can be covered without adding the starter or external services.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No concrete extra dependency, Docker image, environment variable, or system property is required before meaningful spring-boot-actuator 3.0.0 coverage is reachable; the javac failure is caused by Spring Boot 3 API changes from the existing 2.6.x tests, not missing setup.
